location: Dropped by Graveyard Skeleton enemies wielding it around Sellia, Town of Sorcery in Caelid — one patrols the road near the Sellia Backstreets Site of Grace, on the path toward Church of the Plague.
tactics: Skeletons in this area can revive after being knocked down unless finished off cleanly — don't assume a downed one is dead for good.
Drop rate is low (roughly single-digit percent), so plan on farming several kills, resetting at Sellia Backstreets grace.
Nearby Skeletal Executioners can also drop the Glaive at a low chance, giving extra opportunities in the same area.
lore: A long-hafted blade that began as a sickle before being adapted into a weapon of war — its reach and curved edge make it especially effective at sweeping slashes.

builds.name: Early Strength/Dexterity Quality
builds.why: Balanced D/D Strength-Dexterity scaling and decent base attack make it a solid budget quality halberd for early-to-mid Caelid.
